creator: joeb1@a1poweruser.com
text:
I get the following message in the daily security run output on both my 10.1 and 10.2 systems. Both which were installed from scratch using a cdisc1.iso file.

Checking for packages with security vulnerabilities:
pkg: http://vuxml.freebsd.org/freebsd/vuln.xml.bz2: No route to host
pkg: cannot fetch vulnxml file

-- End of security output --


Issuing pkg audit -F from the command line also generates the same error.

Pinging vuxml.freebsd.org works fine so not a network problem. 

When I launch http://vuxml.freebsd.org/freebsd/vuln.xml.bz2 in my browser I get a 404. This means the vuln.xml.bz2 file is not present.
